source_pr.title: planner: avoid adding empty cost formula
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? Issue Number: close #61127 Problem Summary: ### What changed and how does it work? Some cost is created by ```NewZeroCostVer2```. it is an empty formula. so we need to skip it when to print. ### Check List Tests - [x] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No need to test > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed. > Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility Documentation - [ ] Affects user behaviors - [ ] Contains syntax changes - [ ] Contains variable changes - [ ] Contains experimental features - [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility ### Release note Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note. ```release-note None ```

symptoms: EXPLAIN format='cost_trace' output in tpch golden files contained empty cost-formula fragments like `()`, causing mismatches in (tests/integrationtest/r/tpch.result).
failure_signature: tpch.result: EXPLAIN format='cost_trace' output contained empty `()` fragments in costFormula, causing golden mismatch.
root_cause_explanation: The integration test asserted exact `EXPLAIN format='cost_trace'` output (including the costFormula string). Summing costs could append empty formulas created by `NewZeroCostVer2`, producing `() + ...` fragments and breaking the golden comparison. This PR skips empty trace formulas when building the costFormula.
fix_pattern: Skip appending empty cost trace formulas in `SumCostVer2` to stabilize `EXPLAIN format='cost_trace'` golden output.
analysis:
